RE: Reguest for Legal Advice: Mayor Don E. Snith
Dear Mr. Rothman:
Following up our discussion, I am submittingf on behalf of
the City of Orange Mayor Don E. Smith, this reguest for written
advice pursuant to Government Code S 831-14(b). (Mayor Smith,s
authorization is attached. )
Factual Background
In 1985 the City Council received a reconmendation to
authorize a parking study from the Otd Towne Steering Committee,
a Council appointed citizens committee to review the parking
conditions in the OId Towne area of Orange. The purpose of the
study was to determine the existing and long term parking demand
of the area as projected for the year 20L0.
The L00 S. Orange site designated in the study was currently
being used as a municipal parking lot. 
The study recommended
that this existing parking lot be expanded by the construction of
a parking structure. 
The proposed construction would involve a
structure of no more than 35 feet in height, as reguired by the
area's zoning restrictions. 
The structure would add to the
existing 88 stalls an additional 2L2 parking stalls. 
The study
further reconmended the need to improve the l-00 S. Orange parking
lot was motivated by the following factors:
l,) Additional parking will be necessary to accommodate
anticipated land used. However, the projected future land use is
not an increase in development but a conversion from commercial
-retail, 
such as furniture, and antigue stores to a mixed
residential office-professional use.
ORANGE CIVIC CENTER
3OO E. CHAPMAN AVE.
oRANGE, CA 92666-1 591
P.O. BOX 449


Letter to Mr. Rothman
Re: Request for Advice - I'tayor snrith
May 1.1, 1990
Page Two
2t

and
enhance the parking opportunj,ty for locating senior Citizen
Housing Development in the Ol,d Towne Area.
The conctusions regarding the l-00 s. orange parking 1ot
inprovernent have been affinned by the City Council. The next
step is to undertake decisions on the design and development of
the improvement.
llayor Don E. Snith is presently refraining from any
participation in this parking irnprovement both at the council
subconmittee and on the city council level based on his concern
of a possible financial effect from the improvement on certain
real estate that he owns.
As to these properties, for clarity I have arranged below
the properties by their proxirnity to the boundary of the intended
public parking improvenent along with each properties' charac-
teristics, 
income stream and parking availability. 
Please note
that the distance has been calculated based on the nurnber of feet
traveled by a straight 1ine. If the distance is rneasured by
traversing the streets, the distance should be increased by
approximatety 200 feet for each property:

report.

nent and availabl-e for its highest and best use.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Mayor Don E. Smith is required to recuse himself from voting on a proposed public parking structure improvement project at 100 South Orange Street due to his ownership of nearby residential and commercial properties that may be affected by the improvement.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from City Attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion section present. Includes attached authorization from Mayor Smith and MacMillin & Associates appraisal report. Document is formatted with old-style letterhead and formatting typical of 1990 correspondence.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The issue to be resolved is whether Mayor Smith is precluded from participation regarding the 100 South Orange parking improvement due to his ownership of any one of the above listed properties.
embedding.first_500_words: CITY OF ORAI{GEII,, r, ': o. - so iil ,g0 c tTY ATTORN EY -(7 1 4) 532-035 1 FAX (71415324437 May L4, L990 Mr. John Rothman, Esg. Legal Division Fair Political Practices Commission 428 J. Street, Suite 800 Sacrarnento, CA 958L4 RE: Reguest for Legal Advice: Mayor Don E. Snith Dear Mr. Rothman: Following up our discussion, I am submittingf on behalf of the City of Orange Mayor Don E. Smith, this reguest for written advice pursuant to Government Code S 831-14(b). (Mayor Smith,s authorization is attached. ) Factual Background In 1985 the City Council received a reconmendation to authorize a parking study from the Otd Towne Steering Committee, a Council appointed citizens committee to review the parking conditions in the OId Towne area of Orange. The purpose of the study was to determine the existing and long term parking demand of the area as projected for the year 20L0. The L00 S. Orange site designated in the study was currently being used as a municipal parking lot. The study recommended that this existing parking lot be expanded by the construction of a parking structure. The proposed construction would involve a structure of no more than 35 feet in height, as reguired by the area's zoning restrictions. The structure would add to the existing 88 stalls an additional 2L2 parking stalls. The study further reconmended the need to improve the l-00 S. Orange parking lot was motivated by the following factors: l,) Additional parking will be necessary to accommodate anticipated land used. However, the projected future land use is not an increase in development but a conversion from commercial -retail, such as furniture, and antigue stores to a mixed residential office-professional use. ORANGE CIVIC CENTER 3OO E. CHAPMAN AVE. oRANGE, CA 92666-1 591 P.O. BOX 449 Letter to Mr. Rothman Re: Request for Advice - I'tayor snrith May 1.1, 1990 Page Two 2t Improving the existing parking 1ot would facilitate and enhance the parking opportunj,ty for locating senior Citizen Housing Development in the Ol,d Towne Area. The conctusions regarding the l-00 s. orange parking 1ot inprovernent have been affinned by the City Council. The next step is to undertake decisions on the design and development of the improvement. llayor Don E. Snith is presently refraining from any participation in this parking irnprovement both at the council subconmittee and on the city council level based on his concern of a possible financial effect from the improvement on certain real estate that he owns. As to these properties, for clarity I have arranged below the properties by their proxirnity to the boundary of the intended public parking improvenent along with each properties' charac- teristics, income stream and parking availability. Please note that the distance has been calculated based on the nurnber of feet traveled by a straight 1ine. If the distance is rneasured by traversing the streets, the distance should be increased by approximatety 200 feet for each property: L) 254. 254 )./2. 256 l/2 North Grand StreeL a) 1026 feet
embedding.summary: City Attorney Luis A. Rodriguez submits a request for FPPC legal advice on behalf of Mayor Don E. Smith regarding whether the Mayor must recuse himself from participating in decisions on a proposed parking structure improvement in Orange's Old Towne area due to his ownership of several nearby properties. The request includes an independent real estate appraisal opinion concluding that the parking improvement will not impact the market value of the Mayor's properties.
